meaning: adv.不能挽回地,不能恢复地
examples: Her heart was irreparably damaged by a virus.
The fragile economies of several southern African nations could be irreparably damaged.
Today's economic slump could well generate a similar backlog of couples whose relationships have been irreparably ruined.
"The parties would be irreparably harmed by any breach of the agreement, " the statement said.
The inescapable reality that the nomination process has become irreparably broken was made painfully clear last night.
